content: Standard lightweight approach for pixel-to-real-world calibration: two-click distance measurement on a known reference object (ruler) using OpenCV mouse callback (cv2.setMouseCallback) to record two pixel coordinates spanning a known real-world length (e.g. 1 cm mark to 1 cm mark on the ruler), then pixels_per_cm = pixel_distance / known_cm_distance. This is the community-standard 'reference object' calibration approach (PyImageSearch pattern), simpler than ArUco markers or homography/perspective warping which are overkill unless the camera angle is heavily oblique. No dedicated library needed - matplotlib ginput() or a bare OpenCV click handler is sufficient and standard.
